global.title: Global Portfolio Heatmap
global.description: NPS manages a combined equity portfolio across Korean domestic and US markets. This heatmap shows the relative size of each holding. Blue for US stocks, red for Korean stocks.
global.disclaimer: US holdings are sourced from SEC 13F filings (US-listed securities only). Korean holdings are from NPS annual disclosures. This does not represent the full NPS overseas portfolio, which also includes European, Japanese, and other international markets not shown here. Values are as of year-end.
global.estimated: KR data for latest year is estimated from annual disclosure + DART filings. Actual values may differ.

compare.uniqueStocks: total unique stocks across all 5 funds
compare.consensusDesc: Stocks appearing in the top 50 of 4 or more funds

compare.disclaimer: 13F filings only cover US-listed securities. ABP (Netherlands) files through APG with a REIT-focused portfolio. Japan's GPIF is not included.

backtest.title: Backtest: Following NPS's Top Buys
backtest.description: What if you bought the 20 stocks NPS was most aggressively accumulating each quarter? This backtest tests exactly that — buying equal-weight positions in the top 20 stocks by share-increase rate, held from one filing date to the next.
backtest.note: This backtest covers US-listed stocks only (via SEC 13F filings). Korean domestic holdings are not included.
backtest.strategyDesc: Buy top 20 US stocks where NPS increased shares the most (by dollar invested or share increase rate).
backtest.rule1: Each quarter, identify the top 20 US-listed stocks where NPS increased share count the most (by percentage) vs the previous quarter.
backtest.rule2: Buy equal-weight positions on the first business day after the 13F filing date.
backtest.rule3: Hold until the next 13F filing date + 1 business day, then rebalance.
backtest.rule4: Benchmark against SPY (S&P 500 ETF) over the same holding periods.
backtest.disclaimer: This backtest is for educational and informational purposes only. It does not constitute investment advice. Past performance does not guarantee future results. The backtest uses historical 13F filing data and assumes execution at closing prices on the specified dates. Real-world results would differ due to transaction costs, slippage, liquidity constraints, and the 45-day delay between quarter-end and 13F filing deadlines.

strategy.title: NPS Momentum Following
strategy.subtitle: Strategy & Live Tracking
strategy.description: A rules-based investment strategy derived from Korea's National Pension Service portfolio data.
strategy.keyDifferences: Key Differences: Backtest vs Paper vs Live
strategy.liveDesc: Live results will be published here with full transparency — including losses.
strategy.paperDesc: Paper trading results will be updated quarterly.
strategy.howStep1: Every quarter, NPS files 13F with SEC (publicly available)
strategy.howStep2: We identify the top 30 stocks where NPS increased investment the most
strategy.howStep3: Filter: only buy stocks with positive 3-month price momentum
strategy.howStep4: Partial rebalance: keep winning positions, replace losers
strategy.disclaimer: This page is for informational and educational purposes only. Nothing here constitutes investment advice. All investment strategies carry risk, including the potential loss of principal. Always do your own research and consult a qualified financial advisor.

about.title: About NPS & This Tracker
about.npsDesc1: The National Pension Service (NPS) is South Korea's mandatory public pension fund, established in 1988. It is the world's 3rd largest pension fund by assets under management, behind only Japan's GPIF and Norway's Government Pension Fund Global.
about.npsDesc2: NPS manages over $1 trillion (KRW 1,100+ trillion) in total assets as of 2025. The fund covers approximately 22 million contributors — nearly half of South Korea's population — and provides old-age pensions, disability benefits, and survivor benefits.
about.npsDesc3: NPS allocates across domestic and international equities, fixed income, alternative investments (real estate, infrastructure, private equity), and other asset classes. Its overseas equity allocation has been steadily increasing, with US stocks representing the largest single country exposure.
about.f13Desc: SEC Form 13F is a quarterly report required by the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission from institutional investment managers who exercise discretion over $100 million or more in qualifying assets. Filed within 45 days after each calendar quarter end, it discloses long positions in US-listed equities only.
f13Points: Filed within 45 days after each calendar quarter end
Discloses long positions in US-listed equities and equity options
Does not show short positions, non-US stocks, bonds, or most derivatives
Represents a snapshot of holdings at quarter-end
NPS's CIK number is 0001608046 on SEC EDGAR
about.usHoldingsDesc: NPS's US equity holdings from SEC Form 13F quarterly filings (CIK 0001608046). Filed within 45 days after each quarter end. US-listed long positions only.
about.coverageGapDesc: This site's US stock data is based solely on NPS's own 13F filing (CIK 0001608046). As of year-end 2024, this covers 550 stocks totaling ~$105.7B. However, according to NPS's official disclosure (fund.nps.or.kr), their total overseas equity portfolio holds 3,248 stocks worth ~$289B. The 13F data shown here represents roughly 37% of NPS's total overseas equity holdings.
about.coverageGapReasons: Possible reasons for the difference (not officially confirmed by NPS):
coverageGapPoints: The fund.nps.or.kr figure includes all overseas markets (Europe, Japan, etc.) — not just US-listed stocks
Portions of NPS's US equity may be managed by external sub-advisors who file their own separate 13F filings
Indirect holdings through ETFs or pooled funds may be reported differently
about.coverageGapNote: The exact breakdown is not publicly disclosed by NPS. This site tracks only what NPS directly reports to the SEC.
about.krHoldingsDesc: Korean domestic holdings from data.go.kr ("국민연금공단 국내주식 투자정보"). NPS discloses full domestic portfolio annually (~1,200 stocks). 5% ownership threshold triggers additional DART filings.
about.fxDesc: KRW/USD conversion uses yfinance (KRW=X) rate at the relevant reporting date.
about.portfolioDesc: Global portfolio merges US 13F quarterly + Korean annual data, converted to USD at reporting-date exchange rate.
about.backtestDesc: Strategy simulation buying top share-increase stocks from each 13F filing using historical closing prices.
about.whyDesc: NPS is one of the most sophisticated institutional investors in the world, investing with a multi-decade horizon. Their $135B+ US equity portfolio can move markets, and their investment decisions are made by professional analysts with extensive research resources.
about.whyLongTerm: Long-term perspective — As a pension fund, NPS invests with a multi-decade horizon
about.whyResearch: Diversified research — Access to both Korean and global research teams
about.whyImpact: Market impact — With $135B+ in US equities, their buying and selling can move markets
about.whyAngle: Unique angle — As a Korean institution, NPS may have differentiated views
about.limDesc: This site is for informational purposes only and does not constitute investment advice. It is not affiliated with NPS.
limPoints: US stock data is from NPS's own SEC 13F filing (CIK 0001608046) only — this covers ~550 stocks ($105.7B), roughly 37% of NPS's total overseas equity ($289B per fund.nps.or.kr). The remaining holdings are not included in this site
The exact reason for the gap is not officially disclosed by NPS. Likely factors include non-US stocks, delegated management, and indirect holdings
13F filings have a 45-day delay — positions may not reflect current holdings
Korean domestic data is annual (data.go.kr) — supplemented by DART 5%+ stake change tracking
13F covers only US-listed long equity — bonds, derivatives, shorts excluded
Exchange rate conversions introduce minor period-to-period discrepancies
Backtest results are hypothetical — no transaction costs, slippage, or taxes
